content: The incomplete search-based knowledge fragmentation in both topics, where repeated searches for 'agent' leave only disjointed partial results, maps directly to how intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) measure prediction error as curiosity reward: just as curious agents explore unvisited, uncertain state spaces to reduce prediction error, AI researchers implicitly use curiosity-driven reinforcement learning logic when investigating incomplete, fragmented research results, exploring unobserved connections to resolve uncertainty. This frames the research inquiry process itself as a curious agent acting on a knowledge environment.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
